careers.title: Software Engineer
careers.description: Design, develop, and maintain software applications and systems. Work with programming languages, frameworks, and tools to solve technical problems.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related field

careers.title: Data Scientist
careers.description: Analyze and interpret complex data to help organizations make better decisions. Use statistical methods, machine learning, and data visualization techniques.
careers.education: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Statistics, or Computer Science

careers.title: UX/UI Designer
careers.description: Create user-friendly and visually appealing digital products. Research user needs, design wireframes, prototypes, and collaborate with developers.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Design, Human-Computer Interaction, or related field

careers.title: Medical Doctor
careers.description: Diagnose and treat illnesses, injuries, and medical conditions. Provide preventive care and guide patients toward healthier lifestyles.
careers.education: MBBS / MD degree followed by specialization (residency & fellowship)

careers.title: Teacher / Educator
careers.description: Educate students in a subject area, develop curriculum, and create a positive learning environment. Can specialize in primary, secondary, or higher education.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Education or a subject area + teaching certification

careers.title: Business Analyst
careers.description: Bridge the gap between business needs and technology solutions. Analyze processes, gather requirements, and recommend improvements.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Finance, or Computer Science

careers.title: Graphic Designer
careers.description: Create visual content including logos, advertisements, branding materials, and digital graphics. Communicate ideas through typography, imagery, and color.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, or related field
careers.jobGrowth: 3% (Slower than average, but strong freelance opportunities)

careers.title: Cybersecurity Analyst
careers.description: Protect computer systems and networks from cyber threats. Monitor for security breaches, investigate incidents, and implement security measures.
careers.education: Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity, Computer Science, or Information Technology

questions.text: Which subject do you enjoy the most?
How do you prefer to work?

options.label: Alone, focusing deeply on a problem
With a small team on a project
With many people, helping them directly
Freely and creatively with minimal structure

questions.text: What kind of problems do you like solving?
What motivates you most in a career?

options.label: Making a positive impact on society

questions.text: How comfortable are you with technology?

options.label: Very comfortable – I love building or using tech
Comfortable – I use it as a tool
Neutral – I use it when needed

questions.text: Which activity sounds most appealing to you?
